reason: The statement is contradicted by the Israelites (בְנֵֽי־ יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל - regular people, not elite) who are explicitly given dialogue in this passage. They speak directly to Moses, saying 'Hath the LORD brought us forth only to slay us in the wilderness?' and 'Were it not better for us to serve the Egyptians?' The Israelites are common people, not members of the elite class, yet they are given multiple lines of direct speech. While it is true that named individual characters with dialogue (Pharaoh, Moses, God) are elite figures, regular people as a collective are given voice and agency in the narrative through their dialogue.
